key_points: The story is written by H.G. Wells and belongs to science fiction
Griffin is a brilliant but irresponsible scientist
He becomes invisible by using scientific experiments
Invisibility creates practical problems like hunger, cold, and loneliness
Griffin misuses his power for theft and violence
The story shows how power without ethics leads to destruction
characters: Griffin – a genius scientist who misuses science
Mrs Hall – initially kind but later suspicious innkeeper’s wife
Mr Jaffers – the constable who tries to arrest Griffin
Villagers – represent fear of the unknown
important_incidents: Griffin setting fire to his landlord’s house
Footprints appearing without a visible person
Griffin hiding in a departmental store
Strange movements of furniture at the inn
values_and_messages: Scientific power must be guided by moral values
exam_tips: Explain invisibility as a scientific idea, not magic
Link incidents to the theme of misuse of science
Mention Griffin’s moral failure in character answers
Use examples from the story to justify themes
